INDIA POWER SECTOR FY27 OUTLOOK

Demand Outlook
- FY27 electricity demand growth projected at 5% to 5.5%  
- Sharp recovery from around 1% growth in FY26  
- FY26 demand impacted by weather related disruptions and lower thermal utilisation  

Key Demand Drivers
- Higher agricultural and household consumption due to possible sub-par rainfall and El Niño risk  
- Rising industrial activity  
- Growing EV charging infrastructure demand  
- Increasing electricity consumption from data centres  

Thermal Power Revival
- Around 50 GW generation capacity addition expected in FY27  
- Includes nearly 6 GW thermal capacity; balance from renewables  
- Under-construction thermal capacity crossed 45 GW after years of subdued investment  

Thermal PLF Trend
- FY21: 54.5%  
- FY24: 69.1%  
- FY25: 69.5%  
- FY26: 65.7%  
- FY27E: ~65.5%  

- PLFs expected to remain stable despite demand rebound due to fresh thermal additions and renewable penetration  

Investment and Project Activity
- PSU and private sector thermal project announcements increasing  
- State discoms restarting long-term power purchase bidding activity  
- Thermal power regaining importance as reliable base-load generation for grid stability  

Execution Challenges
- Long project gestation periods remain a key risk  
- Boiler, turbine and generator manufacturers operating with elevated order books  

Electricity Pricing Trends
- Average IEX DAM spot tariff declined to ₹3.8/unit in FY26 from ₹4.4/unit in FY25  
- Softer prices due to slower demand growth and higher renewable generation  

Coal and Fuel Position
- Coal stock levels at thermal plants remain comfortable at around 19 days  
- Supported by improved domestic coal availability  

Discom Financial Stress
- Aggregate discom debt declined modestly to ₹7.1 lakh Cr  
- Cash gap still elevated at 30–33 paise per unit  
- Tariff hikes across states remain muted  
- Tariff orders issued in only 17 of 28 states by April 2026  

Key Structural Concerns
- Rising power purchase costs  
- Slow smart metering implementation  
- Weak operational efficiency in distribution segment  
- Limited tariff pass-through mechanisms  

Sector Theme
- India’s power sector is entering a dual-cycle phase:
  - Strong generation demand recovery led by EVs, industry and data centres  
  - Continued financial stress at discom level limiting sector-wide monetisation efficiency  

Final Takeaway
- Power generation and equipment ecosystem likely to benefit from rising demand and thermal revival, while structural discom weakness remains the biggest long-term risk for sector sustainability  

Impact
- Positive for generation, transmission and equipment players  
- Cautious on distribution segment

KPI GREEN ENERGY Q4 HIGHLIGHTS

Capacity Expansion
- Total portfolio expanded from 3.91 GW in FY25 to 6.26 GW in FY26  
- Includes installed and work-in-progress capacity  

Segment-wise Capacity

IPP Segment
- Installed capacity: 0.96 GW  
- WIP capacity: 1.61 GW  

CPP Segment
- Installed capacity: 0.66 GW  
- WIP capacity: 3.03 GW  

Major Order Wins
- 300 MW Wind Power Project from SJVN  
- 120 MW / 240 MWh IPP project from GUVNL  
- 445 MW / 890 MWh BESS order  
- 150 MW Wind Power Project with GUVNL  
- 1 TPD Plasma Green Hydrogen plant  
- 142 MW Floating Solar Photovoltaic project  
- 250 MW Solar Power Project from SJVN  
- 1.13 GW Solar Power Project from Adani Group  

Strategic Positioning
- Increasing focus on:
  - Utility scale renewable projects  
  - Government linked contracts  
  - Advanced clean energy infrastructure  
  - Strategic energy transition opportunities  

Future Growth Areas
- Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS)  
- Energy trading licences  
- Green hydrogen projects  
- Floating solar projects  
- Offshore wind power  
- Pumped storage projects  

Business Transformation
- Evolving from solar EPC player to vertically integrated renewable energy and infrastructure platform  
- Building capabilities across generation, storage and energy solutions ecosystem  

Final Takeaway
- Strong order inflow, diversified renewable pipeline and expansion into storage and hydrogen position KPI Green as an emerging integrated clean energy platform  

Impact
- Positive

WAAREE ENERGY STRATEGIC EXPANSION UPDATE

Long-Term Vision
- Management targeting revenue of ₹1,00,000 Cr within the next 5 years  
- FY26 revenue base around ₹26,500 Cr  
- Transition underway from solar module manufacturer to integrated clean energy infrastructure company  

Business Transformation
- Earlier focus primarily on solar modules  
- Now scaling across:
  - Solar glass  
  - Wafers  
  - Cells  
  - Batteries  
  - Energy Storage Systems (ESS)  
  - Inverters  
  - Hydrogen equipment  

Strategic Goal
- Aim to capture nearly 90% of customer wallet share across the clean energy value chain  
- Building vertically integrated ecosystem to improve margins, scale and customer stickiness  

US Expansion Opportunity
- US market emerging as a major growth driver due to:
  - IRA incentives  
  - Rising AI and data center power demand  
  - Industrial electrification trends  

Capacity Targets
- Targeting 15.4 GW cell manufacturing capacity  
- Targeting 20 GWh battery business by 2028  

Industry Positioning
- Moving beyond solar manufacturing into broader energy infrastructure and storage ecosystem  
- Positioned to benefit from:
  - Renewable energy growth  
  - Grid-scale storage demand  
  - Decarbonization initiatives globally  

Final Takeaway
- Waaree is pursuing an aggressive full-stack clean energy integration strategy with significant optionality across solar, storage and energy infrastructure segments  

Impact
- Positive

Escorts Kubota: Guidance - Concall Update

• Management expects the tractor industry for FY27 to be flattish, with a potential range of 2-3% up or down, anticipating a subdued H2 due to high base effects, subnormal monsoon forecasts, and El Nino impact.

• The company is confident of achieving positive growth and market share improvement in tractor volumes for FY27, irrespective of the industry outlook, driven by recent product launches and channel development.

• The non-tractor agri segment (farm implements) is expected to grow at a 20%+ CAGR over the next three years, supported by product pipeline expansion and channel growth.

• Commodity cost increases are anticipated to be around 5-6% as a percentage of revenue, with a 1.5% price hike already implemented on tractors and construction equipment, which is insufficient to cover the expected increases.

• Normal CapEx for FY27 is projected at INR350-400 crores, with an additional INR500 crores for the greenfield facility this year, part of a larger INR5,000+ crore investment over 7-10 years.

• An additional INR500 crores will be invested in the captive finance division over the next 12-15 months, following an initial INR200 crore investment, with a long-term ROA aspiration of 1.5-2%.

• The Construction Equipment segment anticipates short-term challenges due to macro factors but expects a turnaround in H2 FY27 and strong medium-term demand from public infrastructure development.

Dabur India: Guidance - Concall Update

• Management revised their FY27 revenue growth guidance from high single-digit to low double-digit, anticipating growth from both pricing and volume.

• They expect sequential acceleration of growth in the India business, driven by stable consumption, GTM transformation, premiumization, and brand investments.

• Margins are expected to improve year-on-year, with inflation mitigation through price increases, product mix, savings, and premiumization initiatives.

• The HPC portfolio is expected to achieve double-digit growth in the next year, with oral care specifically targeted for strong double-digit growth in Q1.

• Food and Beverages are targeted for double-digit growth for the full year, contingent on favorable weather conditions.

Blue Star: Guidance - Concall Update

Management is cautiously optimistic for FY27, with the outlook heavily dependent on the summer season's duration and intensity.

For Segment 1 (Electro-Mechanical Projects and Commercial Air Conditioning), the outlook for Commercial AC is 8% to 10% growth, driven by manufacturing, while data center MEP business is expected to more than double within three years, potentially growing from INR1,000 crore to INR3,000 crore annually.

 The company aims to maintain Segment 1 margins at 7% to 7.5% and Segment 2 (Unitary Products) margins at 8% to 8.5% for the current financial year, despite anticipated margin pressure from rising input costs and volatile exchange rates.

Annual capital expenditure is projected to be in the range of INR250 crores to INR350 crores, covering routine CapEx, R&D, product development, and IT/digital investments.

The Room Air Conditioner industry is expected to grow at an 18% to 20% CAGR, potentially reaching 40 million to 50 million units by 2030, with FY27 projected at 17.5 million units, but margins will remain under pressure due to competition and commodity prices.

Radico Khaitan: Guidance - Concall Update

Co achieved net revenue exceeding INR6,000 crores and EBITDA crossing INR1,000 crores in FY'26, with the luxury portfolio delivering INR475 crores in sales value, in line with guidance.

For FY'27, the company expects its luxury portfolio to sustain 25% growth and the Prestige & Above portfolio volume to grow by 20%.

EBITDA margin is projected to expand by 125 basis points for the full year FY'27, driven by price increases (60 bps) and product premiumization (over 200 bps), despite potential commodity cost impacts.

Co aims to become debt-free in FY'27 and has articulated a dividend policy with a minimum payout of 20% of profit after tax.

For the regular segment, a volume growth of 3% to 5% is expected for FY'27, while the non-IMFL business is anticipated to grow at 7% to 8%.

L&T : SECURES SIGNIFICANT EPC ORDER FROM BCGCL FOR COAL-TO-CHEMICALS PROJECT ⚙️🧪

• 🏗️ L&T Energy Hydrocarbon Onshore secured a significant EPC order from Bharat Coal Gasification and Chemicals Ltd (BCGCL)

• 🤝 BCGCL is a joint venture of Coal India Ltd (CIL) and Bharat Heavy Electricals Ltd (BHEL)

• 🧪 Order is for a Coal-to-Ammonium-Nitrate project in Odisha

• ⚡ Project strengthens L&T’s leadership in clean syngas-based ammonia synthesis & coal-to-chemicals EPC segment

• 🏭 L&T will execute the Ammonia Synthesis Unit on an LSTK (Lump Sum Turnkey) basis with single-point responsibility

• 📦 Scope includes: ◦ Process license & basic design
◦ Detailed engineering & procurement
◦ Construction & commissioning
◦ Performance guarantee test runs
◦ Final project handover

• 🌱 Project aimed at enabling downstream manufacturing of nitric acid & ammonium nitrate while improving operational reliability

• 🇮🇳 Management said the project aligns with India’s self-reliance agenda and strengthens indigenous ammonia capacity through coal-based routes

🟢 Impact: Positive (Large EPC order strengthens L&T’s industrial order pipeline and reinforces leadership in complex energy & chemical infrastructure projects)

ONE 97 COMMUNICATIONS MANAGEMENT GUIDANCE UPDATE

FY27 Outlook
- Management expects revenue growth acceleration in FY27  
- Growth to be driven by:
  - Payments business momentum  
  - Financial services expansion  
  - Recovery in marketing services  

Profitability Targets
- Targeting EBITDA margins of 15% to 20% over the next 2.5 to 3 years  
- Operating leverage expected to improve steadily  

Cost Structure
- Indirect costs expected to grow significantly lower than:
  - Revenue growth  
  - Contribution profit growth  

- Focus remains on improving efficiency and scalability  

Customer Acquisition Strategy
- Promotional and cashback spending to continue in a calibrated manner  
- Incentives will remain within existing ratio framework  
- Focus on:
  - High quality customer acquisition  
  - User engagement improvement  

AI Strategy
- Incremental investments planned in AI capabilities  
- AI to enhance customer and merchant experience across existing businesses  
- No major capital expenditure planned for data centers  

Management View
- Strong long-term confidence in payments and financial services ecosystem  
- AI driven operational enhancement expected to support profitability and engagement  

Final Takeaway
- Paytm is focusing on profitable growth through operating leverage, AI integration and scaling financial services while maintaining disciplined customer acquisition spending  

Impact
- Positive

A strong 4Q, with PAT at Rs 7.3bn (+41% YoY) and a best-in-class RoA of 2.1%. 

NIM rose 26bp QoQ to 4.25% (+20bp YoY), driving NII growth of 25% YoY.

 However, mgmt flagged rising NIM pressure from deposit repricing (~40bp hike in 1–3-year FDs in Apr) and lending competition.

 It now guides for FY27 NIM of 3.75–3.80% (vs 3.97% in FY26) and loan growth at 1-2% above industry. 

Competitive intensity led to softer loan growth in 4Q (1.7% QoQ), with commercial loans flat QoQ. 

AQ remained healthy; higher credit cost in the quarter (1.1% vs 0.8% in 4Q last year) was driven by Rs 1.6bn of buffer provisions. 

Our FY27/28E EPS is largely unchanged; we continue to factor in 17% loan growth and 18% RoE over FY27–28E. KVB’s stock has significantly

outperformed over the past year (81% vs 2.6% for Bank Nifty), and valuations at 1.6x

FY28E BV appear stretched.

SAMMAAN CAPITAL GETS RBI NOD FOR NBFC BUSINESS RESTRUCTURING ✅🏦

• 🏛️ RBI has granted ‘No Objection Certificate’ (NOC) for transfer of NBFC business of Sammaan Finserve to Sammaan Capital under proposed scheme of arrangement

• 🔄 Scheme involves restructuring between: • Sammaan Capital Ltd (Resulting Company)
• Sammaan Finserve Ltd (Demerged Company)

• 📌 Company said RBI approval marks a key regulatory milestone for implementation of the scheme

• ✅ Earlier, company had already received: • ‘No adverse observations’ from BSE
• ‘No objection’ from NSE

• ⚖️ Scheme still subject to approvals from: • NCLT
• Shareholders
• Creditors
• Other statutory/regulatory authorities

🟢 Impact: Positive (Major regulatory approval advances restructuring process and strengthens business reorganization plans)

reports.text:
NRB BEARINGS : CO TO INVEST IN 2 PRECISION ENGINEERING COMPANIES ⚙️🏭

• 🛠️ NRB Bearings to invest in: • Mahant Tool Room Private Limited
• NRB Unitech Friction Solutions Private Limited

• ✈️ Mahant Tool Room operates in manufacturing of precision machined components for aerospace industry

• 🔩 NRB Unitech Friction Solutions engaged in cylindrical roller bearings, linear motion products & other precision industrial components

• ₹67 Cr in NRB Unitech through equity & debt infusion (minimum 75% holding)

• 📈 Company said investment strengthens its precision engineering & industrial bearings business capabilities

• ⏳ Acquisition completion timeline: • Mahant Tool Room – within 3 months
• NRB Unitech – within 1 year

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens NRB’s presence in aerospace & precision industrial components segment)

PNC INFRATECH – PROJECT UPDATE

• Declared L1 bidder for Lucknow flyover EPC project by Lucknow Development Authority

• Project includes construction of 4-lane flyover with 2 loops & 2 ramps at Shaheed Path Intersection, Gomti River RHS bank

• Quoted bid value at ₹194.4 Cr

• Execution timeline set at 24 months

• Order strengthens infrastructure order book & revenue visibility

DREDGING CORPORATION – GREEN ENERGY UPDATE

• Commissioned 150 kW rooftop solar plant at Visakhapatnam headquarters under PM Surya Ghar Yojana

• Plant expected to generate ~22,500 units per month and ~2.7 lakh units annually

• Estimated annual power cost savings around ₹30 lakh

• Project expected to reduce 220–240 tonnes of CO2 emissions annually

• Initiative supports sustainability, green energy adoption & operational efficiency

COCHIN SHIPYARD – ORDER WIN

• Udupi Cochin Shipyard (wholly owned subsidiary) secured ‘Notable’ domestic order from Ocean Sparkle Ltd (Adani Group)

• Order involves construction of 4 ASD Tugboats with 70T Bollard Pull capacity

• Vessels to be built under approved standard tug design norms of Ministry of Ports, Shipping & Waterways

• Deliveries scheduled from November 2028 to July 2029

• Order falls under ‘Notable’ category, implying value between ₹100–250 Cr

• Order strengthens long-term order book visibility in specialised shipbuilding segment

MARAL OVERSEAS TO ACQUIRE 26% STAKE IN SOLAR POWER SPV ☀️⚡

• 🌞 Maral Overseas to acquire 26% equity stake in Asawata Energy Pvt Ltd, a solar power SPV

• 💰 Investment size at ₹2.60 lakh under captive power model

• ⚡ Move aimed at optimising power costs & increasing renewable energy share in overall energy mix

• 🤝 Remaining 74% stake to be held by Pickrenew Energy Ltd

• 📜 Long-term Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) to be executed for solar power procurement

• 🏭 SPV to develop & operate a 5 MW solar power project under MP captive generation regulations

🟢 Impact: Positive (Supports lower energy costs, sustainability goals & renewable energy transition)

PRIME FOCUS: NCLT ADMITS INSOLVENCY PLEA AGAINST CO UNDER IBC ⚖️📉

• 🏛️ NCLT Mumbai admitted insolvency petition filed by Reliance Alpha Services under Section 7 of IBC against Prime Focus

• 💰 Petition alleges financial debt of ₹353.79 crore, including interest, linked to a 2019 loan agreement

• ⚠️ Company said it has already moved NCLAT seeking urgent relief and stay on NCLT order

• 🗣️ Prime Focus claims no funds were disbursed under the loan agreement and disputes petitioner’s status as a “financial creditor”

• 🔄 Company stated operations continue normally with no disruption to VFX and post-production businesses

reports.text:
Capex Amount for 1GW

Module- 100-150Cr 
Cell+ Module- 500-600Cr
Ingot&Wafer +Cell+ Module- 1100-1200Cr
Poly to Module- 1500-1600Cr 

Capital Intensity increases as you go up from Just Module Manufacturer to Integrated Player.
10x More Capex Heavy than just Module. 

India first incentivesed Module Manufactures, Then Cell and Now moving towards Ingot & Wafer as well. 

Those who will be integrated player, only will be able to catch this train.

PNC INFRATECH EMERGES L1 BIDDER FOR ₹194 CR LUCKNOW FLYOVER PROJECT 🛣️🏗️

• 🏆 PNC Infratech declared L1 (lowest) bidder for EPC flyover project awarded by Lucknow Development Authority, Uttar Pradesh

• 🌉 Project involves construction of 4-lane flyover with 2 loops & 2 ramps at Shaheed Path Intersection, RHS bank of Gomti River, Lucknow

• 💰 Quoted project value stands at ₹194.4 crore

• ⏳ Project to be executed over 24 months

• 🇮🇳 Order awarded by domestic entity under EPC mode

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens order book and improves revenue visibility in infrastructure segment)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
DREDGING CORP COMMISSIONS 150 KW SOLAR PLANT AT HQ ☀️⚡

• 🌞 Dredging Corporation of India commissioned a 150 kW rooftop solar plant at its Visakhapatnam head office under PM Surya Ghar Yojana

• ⚡ Plant expected to generate ~22,500 units/month and ~2.7 lakh units annually of clean energy

• 💰 Estimated annual power cost savings of around ₹30 lakh for the company

• 🌱 Project expected to reduce 220–240 tonnes of CO2 emissions annually

• 🚢 Initiative aligns with Ministry of Ports, Shipping & Waterways’ sustainability and green energy goals

🟢 Impact: Positive (Supports cost optimisation, sustainability goals and operational efficiency)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
COCHIN SHIPYARD: ARM SECURES ‘NOTABLE’ TUG ORDER FROM ADANI GROUP CO 🚢⚓

• 🛳️ Udupi Cochin Shipyard (Udupi-CSL), wholly-owned subsidiary of Cochin Shipyard, won a ‘Notable’ domestic order from Ocean Sparkle Ltd (Adani Group)

• ⚙️ Order includes construction of 4 ASD Tugboats with 70T Bollard Pull power

• 🇮🇳 Tugs to be built as per Ministry of Ports, Shipping & Waterways’ approved standard tug design norms

• 📅 Deliveries to begin from November 2028 and complete by July 2029

• 💰 Company classified the order under ‘Notable’ category, indicating value between ₹100 Cr–₹250 Cr

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens order book visibility and reinforces CSL’s position in specialised shipbuilding segment)

Bajaj Auto (BJAUT) delivered a strong Q4FY26, surpassing expectations with revenue growing 32% YoY to ₹160.1bn and EBITDAM at 20.8% (30bps above estimates). 

The company sees domestic motorcycle industry growth moderating to ~7-9% in the near term due to price hikes and weakening consumer sentiment. 

However, export demand for both 2-wheelers (2Ws) and 3-wheelers (3Ws) remains robust, with BJAUT outperforming industry growth in its top export markets. 🏍

Two interesting points from Paytm’s results:

- Paytm says it is not interested in a balance sheet lending play and will continue to remain a loan distributor for other NBFCs and banks. This comes as rival PhonePe has applied for an NBFC licence for the third time.

- It also wants to retain its $1.4 bn cash balance for acquisitions “at the right price and only where strategically additive.” Paytm has not made any sizable acquisitions since its IPO in 2021. AI-based platforms are one area it is exploring.

*~~Citi*

*India Electric Utilities: Switched-On Capex Cycle: Initiate NTPC, PGCIL, TTPW, JSWE at Buy*

India’s first-ever multi-vector capex upcycle – straddling thermal, renewables, transmission, and grid storage – continues to chug along. It remains a multi-year visible story, with the breadth of power demand gradually expanding. Our medium-term forecast is set at a 5-6% CAGR on the strength of electrification, data centres, cooling load, and policy-supported manufacturing sectors. To boot, regulators’ focus has pivoted from capacity to reliability/flexibility, and favorable policies are providing greater visibility. Against this structural change in backdrop, we initiate at Buy on NTPC (TP Rs485), TTPW (Rs525), PGRD (Rs380), JSWE (Rs650) – in that order of preference.

*Stock views: NTPC > Tata Power >PGRD > JSWE — Although power-sector stocks have re-rated in the past month on a power-demand pickup, we believe they still have upside potential. None are materially exposed to merchant price volatility.*

- *NTPC:* Duration/diversity of its regulated business and its multi-modal generation exposure with a cost of funds advantage position it well.

- *Tata Power:* Multi-year transition, with a growing share of lower-volatility businesses as capex stays focused on T&D and renewables.

- *PGRD:* Direct beneficiary of transmission grid buildout in India. Commissioning and capex trajectories have improved.

- *JSWE:* Well placed to benefit from both thermal and renewable buildout.

*Key risks* — a) Execution slippages; b) sustained unfavorable demand variability; c) adverse regulatory developments.

India’s Energy Edge: Optionality

India’s energy security is no longer about self-sufficiency; it’s about strategic optionality. Importing over 85% of its crude, India has navigated the West Asia crisis by diversifying its basket—shifting from Middle East reliance to making Russia its largest supplier (36%). While Brent crude hovers near $109, India’s ability to pivot between Iraq, Saudi Arabia, and the US has provided a vital macroeconomic cushion.

However, the "new oil" order brings fresh risks. As we transition toward EVs and solar, our dependence moves from oil chokepoints like the Strait of Hormuz to critical mineral supply chains dominated by China. Resilience now requires more than just filling strategic reserves; it demands securing the lithium and cobalt needed for the next decade of growth. Diversification is our only shield in a fragmented market.

reports.text:
SEIL Receives 5-Year Renewal of MES Approval

Steel Exchange India Ltd. has received renewal of approval from the Military Engineer Services (MES) under the Ministry of Defence for supply of SIMHADRI TMT bars for the next 5 years.

Update Highlights:
* Approval renewed for Fe 500D and Fe 500D HCRM TMT bars from 8 mm to 32 mm.
* Products are manufactured at SEIL’s integrated Vizianagaram plant using TEMPCORE technology.
* Renewal follows MES quality checks, technical evaluations, and inspection standards.
* MES approvals are granted only to manufacturers meeting stringent quality requirements.
* Approval remains subject to periodic inspections and IS 1786:2008 compliance.
* SEIL continues to remain eligible for MES-linked infrastructure and defence projects.

Strategic Alignment:
The renewal strengthens SEIL’s position in institutional and government-linked segments while reinforcing its focus on quality standards, operational discipline, and participation in high entry-barrier projects.

reports.text:
Thailand's Land Bridge: A Game-Changer for Global Trade?

Thailand is pushing ahead with its ambitious $30B+ Land Bridge — a 90km multimodal corridor linking the Andaman Sea to the Gulf of Thailand. The goal? Create a faster, cheaper overland shortcut that bypasses the congested and vulnerable Strait of Malacca, shaving 4-5 days off shipping times and ~15% off costs.

In an era of Red Sea/Hormuz disruptions and growing worries about maritime chokepoints, this project isn't just infrastructure — it's strategic resilience. If executed well, it could reposition Thailand as a key logistics hub in the Indo-Pacific, challenging Singapore's dominance and reshaping supply chains.

Decades in discussion, now gaining real momentum. The big questions remain: execution, environment, and geopolitics. Worth watching closely.

Join our WhatsApp Channel. 🙏🏻

👉 https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029VbBm3EiKWEKxIsMdoz2X

Politicians: The Demographic of Disconnect

While the common man pours into SIPs, India’s political class remains allergic to the demat account. Out of ₹29,032 crore in disclosed assets across five states, a staggering 99% is locked in land, buildings, and gold. Only a minuscule 0.97% sits in regulated capital markets—a stark contrast to US Congress members who often hold 50-80% of their wealth in stocks.

The data reveals a "sideline" mentality: only 461 out of 7,325 candidates disclosed any market exposure. For a leadership pushing a "Viksit Bharat" built on financialization, their personal portfolios tell a different story. They aren't betting on India’s corporate growth; they are betting on the soil beneath it. The biggest underserved market for financial advisors? The very people making the policies.

**CLSA on Mahindra & Mahindra**

**Target Price: ₹4,279**  
**Recommendation: Outperform**  

Market share gains and launches drive optimism  

• UV market share rose to ~25.3%  
• Market share improved ~260 bps YoY  
• Strong launch pipeline supports growth  
• Profitability trends improving steadily  
• Conservative tractor outlook due to El Niño  
• Raw material inflation remains concern

**HSBC on Mahindra & Mahindra**

**Target Price: ₹4,200**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Broad launch pipeline supports growth outlook  

• Targets mid-to-high teen SUV growth  
• Launch pipeline expanded significantly  
• Pipeline includes ICE SUVs and BEVs  
• Multiple LCV launches planned ahead  
• Commodity inflation remains key risk  
• Weak monsoon may impact demand

**Nomura on Mahindra & Mahindra**

**Target Price: ₹4,580**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Strong SUV pipeline improves long-term visibility  

• SUV launch pipeline remains robust  
• ICE and EV launches to drive growth  
• FY27/FY28 SUV estimates raised  
• Capacity ramp-up supporting volumes  
• Near-term margin risks still persist

**HSBC on Coforge**

**Target Price: ₹1,710**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Strong order book supports medium-term growth  

• Q4 revenue slightly below expectations  
• FY27 margin guidance improved  
• FCF guidance stronger than expected  
• Exiting low-margin business may impact growth  
• Low-teens growth still achievable  
• Strong order book provides visibility  
• AI-related sector headwinds continue  
• Valuation appears reasonable

**Nomura on Aadhar Housing Finance**

**Target Price: ₹615**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Consistent execution with steady growth outlook  

• Management maintains ~20% FY27 growth guidance  
• AUM and profit growth guided at ~20%  
• Net profit grew ~27% YoY  
• Growth driven by strong AUM expansion  
• Opex moderation supported profitability  
• No impact from West Asia conflict yet  
• RoA/RoE seen at ~4.2–4.3% / 16.5%

**Nomura on Larsen & Toubro**

**Target Price: ₹3,940**  
**Recommendation: Neutral (Downgrade)**  

Subdued operations and weaker outlook concerns  

• Q4 operating performance remained subdued  
• FY27 order inflow outlook less optimistic  
• Lakshya 2031 guidance seen underwhelming  
• FY27 and FY28 earnings estimates cut  
• Earnings estimates reduced by ~8%

**HSBC on Larsen & Toubro**

**Target Price: ₹3,800 (Cut)**  
**Recommendation: Hold**  

Strong execution but growth sustainability questioned  

• Q4 order inflows remained strong  
• Revenue growth healthy during quarter  
• Limited impact from West Asia conflict  
• Elevated base may slow future growth  
• New investments may pressure RoE

reports.text:
**CLSA on Larsen & Toubro**

**Target Price: ₹4,842**  
**Recommendation: Outperform**  

Strong order momentum and backlog growth continue  

• Order inflows grew ~22% YoY in Q4  
• Growth driven by energy and infra orders  
• India private capex orders remained strong  
• Order backlog rose ~28% YoY  
• Backlog reached ~US$82 bn  
• Working capital improved ~690 bps  
• FY27 growth guidance at 10–12%  
• Margin outlook linked to Hormuz reopening

MS on PNB

UW, TP Rs 88

Core revenues were 5% below MSe.

Core PPOP adjusted for reversal of retirement provisions (due to higher bond yields) also missed MSe

Reversal of retirement provisions, lower credit costs (owing to certain lumpy reversals) & bad loan recoveries drove a strong headline PAT beat.

Nomura on Coforge

Buy, TP Rs 2100

Revenue in line; strong beat on margins

Continued improvement in FCF & EBIT margin should allay market concerns

4QFY26 – revenues in line, but significant beat at EBIT margin

Strong, executable order book gives comfort to near- to medium-term revenue outlook

Leveraging on AI for further margin improvement in near to medium term

Raise FY27-28F EPS by ~2-5%

**Jefferies on SRF**

**Target Price: ₹2,640**  
**Recommendation: Underperform**  

Strong quarterly beat driven by chemicals segment  

• EBITDA and PAT beat estimates strongly  
• Chemicals revenue/EBITDA beat by 18%/30%  
• Refgas performance drove earnings upside  
• Export volumes and prices improved sharply  
• Packaging EBIT beat estimates by ~4%  
• Capex doubled for new HFO plant  
• HF capacity expansion also announced  
• HFC capacity increased by ~25%

Why Lloyds Metal is not just another mining company?

A Thread

https://x.com/BeatTheStreet10/status/2051872770280280458?s=20

**CLSA on Punjab National Bank**

**Target Price: ₹135**  
**Recommendation: Outperform**  

Lower costs support earnings despite weak NII  

• PAT beat estimates by ~16%  
• Lower opex and credit costs aided profits  
• NII declined ~3% QoQ  
• Loan growth at ~13.7% YoY  
• Deposit growth slowed to ~9% YoY  
• CASA ratio remained steady at ~36%  
• Gross slippage ratio improved to ~0.9%  
• FY27–28 PAT estimates cut by ~3%

**Kotak Securities on Punjab National Bank**

**Target Price: ₹125 (Earlier ₹140)**  
**Recommendation: Add**  

Stable profitability with improving balance sheet  

• Overall profitability remained steady  
• Lower credit costs supported RoE  
• Retirement-related provisions declined sharply  
• Asset quality trends remained stable  
• High PCR supports lower future credit costs  
• Core profitability still lags peers
Lloyds Metal Guidance for FY27 : Mutlifold Volume Growth across all product verticals

**UBS on Punjab National Bank**

**Target Price: ₹122 (Earlier ₹140)**  
**Recommendation: Neutral**  

Weak margins offset by lower credit costs  

• Weak NII impacted overall performance  
• Q4 PAT beat aided by lower opex  
• Credit costs declined during quarter  
• Loan growth momentum remained modest  
• Margins moderated by ~5 bps QoQ  
• Management guides 12–13% loan growth  
• RoA guidance maintained above 1%

**JP Morgan on Mahindra & Mahindra**

**Target Price: ₹4,135 (Earlier ₹3,980)**  
**Recommendation: Overweight**  

SUV momentum driving stronger growth outlook  

• FY27 guidance ahead of expectations  
• Better guidance may support stock performance  
• Upgrade driven by SUV volume strength  
• Margin headwinds expected across businesses

**Kotak Securities on Mahindra & Mahindra**

**Target Price: ₹4,050 (Earlier ₹4,000)**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Strong execution and product pipeline support outlook  

• Strong execution continues across businesses  
• Better Q4 aided by stronger vehicle ASPs  
• Tractor profitability below expectations  
• Automotive segment performance remained strong  
• Expects outperformance in tractors and CVs  
• SUV launch pipeline strong till CY30E  
• Leadership maintained across key segments  
• Valuations viewed as inexpensive

JPMorgan On Hero Moto

Target Price ₹6385

Recommendation Overweight

Q4 - pricing and fixed cost management offset commodity pressures

No material negative trends in the results

Key questions would be regarding the sustainability of industry growth, market share sustenance and commodity pressures going forward

**Morgan Stanley on Marico**

**Target Price: ₹934**  
**Recommendation: Overweight**  

Positive trends continue into FY27  

• Encouraging Q4 operational performance  
• FY27 commentary remained positive  
• EBITDA growth guidance raised to high-teens  
• Earlier guidance was mid-teens growth  
• Commodity inflation impact currently minimal

**Citi on Marico**

**Target Price: ₹940 (Earlier ₹900)**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Strong Q4 improves medium-term visibility  

• Q4 performance supports growth visibility  
• Medium-term momentum expected to remain robust  
• Falling copra prices support margins  
• Crude-linked inflation partly offset by copra  
• Revenue CAGR estimated at ~11%  
• EPS CAGR estimated at ~16% FY26–29

**Jefferies on Marico**

**Target Price: ₹960 (Earlier ₹900)**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Strong execution continues with healthy growth outlook  

• “Consistent compounder delivers yet again”  
• Management confident about future outlook  
• FY27 EBITDA growth seen in high-teens  
• Mid-teens growth expected through FY30  
• Internal business trends remain strong  
• External factors remain key monitor  
• Continues to remain top sector pick

Goldman Sachs On KEI Industries

Target Price ₹5005

Earlier Target ₹4585

Recommendation Buy

New capacity ramp-up to drive FY27 growth faster than peers

End market demand remains resilient amid capex

Faster growth than industry leader in the medium term to justify premium valuations

Citi on Hero Moto

Target Price ₹6900

Recommendation Buy

Q4 Results: EBITDA slightly ahead of estimates

PAT In-Line due to lower other income

Demand has remained robust post GST cuts

Efforts in boosting market shares in scooters and EVs are gradually fructifying

Cost-reduction initiatives are supporting margins

Possible inflationary macro-environment and any adverse rural income trend could be potential headwinds

Jefferies On M&M

Target Price ₹4000

Earlier Target ₹4500

Recommendation Buy

Strong Q4; Tractor cycle concern, but autos good and valuations reasonable

Delivered 16th consecutive quarter of double-digit EBITDA growth

Improving EV margins, CAFE norm concerns have eased, and new SUV launches will restart in FY28

Cut FY27-28 EPS by 3-5%; now expect 11% core EPS CAGR over FY26-28E, followed by 20% growth in FY29

Jefferies On Coforge

Target Price ₹1860

Earlier Target ₹1620

Recommendation Buy

Improving margin outlook and earnings quality

Q4 results beat estimates with higher-than-expected margins and FCF conversion surprising positively

Strong deal wins and 16% year-on-year growth in executable order book provide visibility

See double-digit organic growth despite clean-up of low-margin India portfolio

Expect 23% EPS CAGR over FY27-29

Valuations at 19x 1-year fwd PE are attractive

Citi on M&M

Target Price ₹4470

Earlier Target ₹4230

Recommendation Buy

Q4 results were above estimates, driven by higher auto ASPs and better gross margin

Management's outlook is positive; UV/tractor growth guidance is at mid-high teens/mid-single digit year-on-year

Increase our tractor and UV volumes, although adverse monsoons could be a potential headwind

Lower margins reflect possible cost pressure

Jefferies On PNB

Target Price ₹130

Earlier Target ₹134

Recommendation Buy

Q4 NII Miss made-up by lower opex and provisions

Loan growth improved, but NIM compression dragged NII

Headroom in LDR allows PNB to grow loans despite soft deposit growth

Slippages fell year-on-year and core credit costs are controlled

Ability to sustain ROA despite transition to ECL will be key

Citi on PNB

Target Price ₹103

Earlier Target ₹115

Recommendation Sell

NIMs contract further; Provision release and recoveries keep RoA afloat

FY27 guidance of 2.6-2.7% implies modest recovery

Slippages tick up quarter-on-quarter; creates further floating provisions

Loan growth below system average as retail lags; MSME and overseas drive growth
Kumar Mangalam Birla back to Vodafone India as Chairman

Jefferies On L&T

Target Price ₹4885

Earlier Target ₹4500

Recommendation Buy

Growth visibility offered in a tough environment

Unveiled its Lakshya 2031 plan, after beating its Lakshya 2026 plan on revenue and order flow CAGR

10-12% order flow CAGR and 12-15% revenue CAGR are the 2031 guidance

This includes material $2 billion investment in data centres, electronics and semiconductors

Real estate IPO - part of Lakshya 2031 value creation

Double-digit medium-term earnings CAGR visibility should drive stock

Jefferies On Emcure

Target Price ₹1970

Earlier Target ₹1780

Recommendation Buy

Q4 beat and an encouraging guidance for FY27

Domestic growth was modest at just 5% year-on-year, impacted by Zuventus integration process & reorganization

FY27 guidance was encouraging with low to mid-teens sales growth and 75-100bps EBITDA margin expansion

Overall, Emcure guided for growth normalization in India and continued momentum in Europe and RoW market

Citi On Banks

ECLGS 5.0 Approved - Rs 2.55tn incremental credit flow to MSMEs

Positive for lenders

Direct policy response to economic disruptions caused by the West Asia Crisis is positive for credit sentiment, asset quality, and financial stability, in our view

Key beneficiaries - MSME-focused lenders including Axis Bank, RBL Bank, PSU banks

All IT co' have reported results

Persistent & Coforge continues to be outliers in the space

Persistent, Coforge, OFSS delivered 20%+ REV growth with solid operating leverage🔥
What FIIs bought and sold in Q4 FY26

reports.text:
GOLDMAN SACHS ON TATA TECHNOLOGIES

Target price revised to ₹470 from ₹450

Q4 results in-line with estimates

Management maintained guidance for double-digit organic revenue growth in FY27

Introduced FY27 EBITDA margin guidance of 18% versus 15.7% in FY26

Brokerage expects margin improvement from better utilization and Al-led efficiencies

Says Middle East conflict unlikely to impact auto OEM capex plans materially

Raises FY27/FY29 EPS estimates by 10%/6%

ELARA CAPITAL ON JYOTHY LABS

Maintain Buy, Target price: ₹335 versus 320

Q4 revenue growth driven by fabric care and personal care segments

Volume growth came in strong at 10.8%

Margins remained under pressure due to crude-linked input cost inflation

Gross margin contracted 407 bps YoY while EBITDA margin declined 335 bps YoY

Brokerage cuts FY27 EPS estimate by 19.3% due to margin pressure

Maintains positive stance citing long-term growth visibility

reports.text:
MORGAN STANLEY ON KEI INDUSTRIES

Downgrade to Equal-weight from Overweight

Target price: ₹5,213 versus ₹4,860

Q4 PAT beat estimates led by stronger margins despite softer volume growth

Domestic C&W revenue grew 23% while exports remained weak

Brokerage sees revenue growth supported largely by higher commodity prices and rupee depreciation

Warns of margin pressure from rising competition in wires segment

Cuts FY27/FY28 EPS estimates by 3-4%

**Jefferies on Ambuja Cement**

**Target Price: ₹465 (Cut)**  
**Recommendation: Neutral**  

Capex delays and cost uncertainty weigh outlook  

• Expansion plans being recalibrated/delayed  
• Capacity growth slower than earlier targets  
• Cost reduction guidance lowered significantly  
• Q4 costs flattish QoQ at ~₹4,500/t  
• West Asia crisis added ~₹250/t cost impact  
• Demand softness may pressure pricing  
• Uncertainty on growth and cost structure  
• Valuations partly reflect these concerns

**Nomura on Cholamandalam Investment**

**Target Price: ₹1,760**  
**Recommendation: Neutral**  

Recovery underway with moderated growth outlook  

• Moving past a challenging year  
• FY27 AUM growth guided at 20–23%  
• Credit cost expected lower by ~10 bps YoY  
• Q4 PAT beat estimates by ~10%  
• Provision overlays created by management  
• FY27 profit estimates raised by ~2%

**Jefferies on Cholamandalam Investment**

**Target Price: ₹1,960**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Strong growth momentum with improving asset quality  

• Q4 PAT ₹16.4 bn, up ~30% YoY  
• Beat estimates by ~11%  
• Higher NII and fees supported earnings  
• Auto disbursals remained robust  
• SME and CSEL segments recovered growth  
• AUM CAGR seen at ~21% FY26–28  
• NIMs expected stable despite macro risks  
• Asset quality improved sharply  
• Credit cost likely to decline further  
• EPS CAGR ~24%, RoE ~20% expected

**CLSA on Godrej Properties**

**Target Price: ₹2,600**  
**Recommendation: Outperform**  

Strong demand outlook with improving cash flow trajectory  

• Strong growth reported in cash flows  
• FY27 presales growth guided at ~14% YoY  
• Growth despite high base in FY26  
• Housing demand remains healthy across markets  
• Collections lagged presales in FY26  
• Collections expected to improve in FY27  
• Sharp cash flow increase expected in FY28  
• Driven by project completions and execution

**CLSA on Manappuram Finance**

**Target Price: ₹305**  
**Recommendation: Hold**  

Earnings miss with yield pressure offsetting growth  

• Q4 PAT missed estimates by ~14%  
• NII missed by ~6%; provisions higher  
• One-off write-off in vehicle finance segment  
• Gold loan growth strong at ~30% QoQ  
• Tonnage growth supported volume expansion  
• Yields declined ~80 bps QoQ to ~17.7%  
• Gold loan yields down ~400 bps in four quarters  
• Management indicates yields have bottomed  
• Reported LTV stable at ~57%

**Jefferies on Aditya Birla Capital**

**Target Price: ₹425**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Diversified growth with improving asset quality  

• Consolidated PAT grew ~29% YoY to ₹11 bn  
• NBFC PAT missed on lower NII, higher opex  
• Asset quality improved; provisions declined  
• Life insurance VNB grew ~14% YoY  
• Premium growth driven by better product mix  
• Health insurance saw strong premium growth  
• Raise LI estimates; cut AMC estimates  
• NBFC AUM CAGR seen at ~26%  
• EPS CAGR ~23% with RoE ~16% by FY28

**HSBC on Godrej Properties**

**Target Price: ₹2,900**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Strong pre-sales outlook with improving cash flows  

• Pre-sales guidance at ₹390 bn, up ~14% YoY  
• Launch pipeline guided to grow ~14% YoY  
• Collections expected to rise ~20% YoY  
• Business development activity to moderate  
• FCFE expected to turn positive in FY27

**HSBC on Ather Energy**

**Target Price: ₹1,050**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Strong EV positioning despite near-term cost pressures  

• Profitability timelines may be delayed  
• Commodity headwinds impacting margins  
• Long-term EV thesis remains intact  
• EV penetration trends remain key driver  
• Company execution remains strong  
• Brand strength supports premium positioning  
• Valuation not cheap but justified

**Coforge on Cigniti Technologies Merger**

**Impact: Positive**  

Amalgamation completed; strengthens digital capabilities  

• NCLT-approved merger now effective  
• Cigniti merged into Coforge entity  
• Appointed date set as April 1, 2025  
• Cigniti dissolved without winding up  
• Financials to be consolidated with Coforge  
• Record date for share swap awaited  
• Shareholders to receive Coforge shares  
• Enhances digital engineering and testing scale

**Jefferies on Manappuram Finance**

Earnings recovery driven by improving NIMs and lower provisions  

• Q4 PAT ₹4 bn, up ~69% QoQ  
• Beat estimates on ECL provision reversal  
• AUM grew ~22% QoQ strongly  
• NIM declined due to lower yields  
• Gold loan growth showing improvement  
• FY26 impacted by NIM pressure, provisions  
• NIMs bottoming, provisions likely peaked  
• Profit to grow ~2.6x over FY26–28  
• RoE to improve to ~13%  
• Valuation at ~1.6x FY27E BV reasonable

**JP Morgan on Tata Technologies**

**Target Price: ₹560**  
**Recommendation: Neutral**  

Strong growth visibility but valuations remain elevated  

• Q4 CC revenue grew ~7.8% QoQ  
• Growth driven by deal ramp-ups  
• JLR demand returned to normal levels  
• FY27 growth guided at ~10%  
• Low CQGR needed to achieve guidance  
• Multiple large deal wins improving visibility  
• Pipeline remains strong for near-term closures  
• Guidance factors Middle East-related headwinds  
• EBITDA margin to rise from 16% to 18%  
• Valuation at ~30x/26x forward PE high

**JP Morgan on BHEL**

**Target Price: ₹220**  
**Recommendation: Underweight**  

Weak order trends and cyclical peak concerns  

• FY26 order inflow declined ~19% YoY  
• Power orders down ~27% YoY  
• Thermal ordering cycle likely peaked  
• Renewables pose long-term risk  
• Energy storage impacting demand outlook  
• Recent rally seen as exit opportunity

reports.text:
*IIFL Sales* 😊😊😊
#Ideaforge

*IdeaForge - UPGRADE to BUY with a TP of Rs 1187*
• Principal approval of 20k cr for drones by Indian Army. 
• Consistent investment in R&D and product devep has put the company back in the race. 
• All financial metrices have witnessed a revival. 
• Institutional holding @ minimal levels since IPO. 
** Would suggest re evaluating given the stock is trading at ~45% disc to peers (35x FY28ii eps)

NOMURA ON FEDERAL BANK

Upgrade from Neutral to Buy with a target price of ₹330 (earlier ₹215)

4QFY26 strong, with consistent execution supporting upgrade

EPS estimates raised ~9%/13% for FY27/FY28, driven by higher fee income and lower opex

Expect ~17% EPS CAGR over FY26-28F, with RoA/RoE at ~1.3%/12% by FY28F

Asset quality remains strong, better than edin mid-tier peers

Core PPOP beat, led by fee income and controlled costs

Healthy loan growth in focus segments, with stable asset quality

GOLDMAN SACHS ON INDIA TELECOM/INDUS TOWERS

Maintain Sell on Indus Towers, Target price unchanged at 365

Core organic revenue and EBITDA growth slowed to 5% YoY in 4QFY26

Brokerage expects growth moderation with Bharti tower rollouts nearing completion

FY27 revenue/EBITDA growth estimated at 5%/7%

Vodafone Idea AGR relief could support incremental tower rollout activity

Highlights potential risks from Africa expansion and customer contract expiry

MORGAN STANLEY ON MANAPPURAM FINANCE

Maintain Equal-weight , Target price unchanged at ₹ 270

Asirvad MFI posted adjusted Q4 profit of 135 mn versus estimate of loss

Lower provisions and ECL model changes aided profitability

GNPA declined to 4.85% while provision coverage improved to 68%

Rights issue strengthened capital adequacy to 31.4%

Awaits outlook on provisions, growth and funding availability

MORGAN STANLEY ON CREDIT GROWTH TRENDS

Industry View: Attractive

Non-food credit growth improved to 15.9% YoY in March 2026

Retail, services, industry and agri segments all saw acceleration

MSME loan growth remained strong at 29.6% YoY

NBFC lending growth accelerated to 26.3% YoY

Gold loan growth remained elevated at 123% YoY

Fortnightly April data indicates moderation in momentum

**Goldman Sachs on Dr Lal PathLabs**

**Target Price: ₹1,300**  
**Recommendation: Sell**  

Strong volumes but margin pressure persists  

• Revenue/EBITDA grew ~16.6%/10.5% YoY  
• Volume growth strong across patients and samples  
• Revenue per patient improved ~7.8% YoY  
• EBITDA margin at ~26.6% YoY  
• Pricing strategy remains calibrated  
• FY27 growth guided at 13–15%  
• Margins expected stable at ~27–28%

**Nomura on Dr Lal PathLabs**

**Target Price: ₹1,860**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Volume-led growth acceleration supports re-rating  

• Q4FY26 growth accelerating across key metrics  
• Revenue beat estimates; EBITDA in line  
• Volume-driven growth to drive valuations higher  
• Valuations near seven-year lows  
• Strong balance sheet supports acquisitions  
• Volume growth remains key re-rating trigger

**HSBC on Kajaria Ceramics**

**Target Price: ₹1,400 (Raised)**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Volume growth supported by industry disruption  

• Q4FY26 beat driven by higher volumes  
• Unorganised segment disruption aided gains  
• Margins improved on operating efficiencies  
• Reduced competition benefits organised players  
• Cost optimisation initiatives yielding results

**HSBC on RR Kabel**

**Target Price: ₹1,770**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Strong W&C demand and margin tailwinds  

• W&C segment drove strong Q4 performance  
• Commodity tailwinds supporting margins  
• Operating leverage aiding profitability  
• FMEG segment turnaround underway  
• ~21% EPS CAGR expected over FY26–29

**HSBC on Aster DM Healthcare**

**Target Price: ₹785**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Improving case mix and expansion to drive growth  

• Q4FY26 strong, led by better case mix  
• Further scope to improve patient mix  
• Retaining top clinical talent remains key  
• Expansion in key markets like Bengaluru  
• Aster-QCIL merger expected in Q1FY27

**Jefferies on Adani Ports**

**Target Price: ₹1,980**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Strong realisations and long-term growth drivers  

• Q4 EBITDA beat by ~9%  
• Domestic port realisations drove performance  
• FY27 EBITDA growth guided at 9–14%  
• Growth driven by volumes and capacity expansion  
• Industrialisation and trade support demand  
• ROCE to expand ~1% annually

**Goldman Sachs on Adani Ports**

**Target Price: ₹1,710**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Logistics growth driving overall performance  

• Revenue broadly in-line with estimates  
• Logistics and marine drove growth strongly  
• Port cargo volumes up ~13% YoY  
• Port margins at ~66.4%  
• Logistics margins improved ~250 bps YoY  
• FY27 revenue growth guided at 11–16%

**Nomura on Adani Ports**

**Target Price: ₹1,930**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Robust outlook with capacity expansion plans  

• Q4 EBITDA beat estimates by ~6%  
• FY27 EBITDA growth guided at 9–14%  
• FY28 EBITDA estimates raised by ~6%  
• Targets ~19% EBITDA CAGR FY26–28  
• Capacity to reach ~1,000 MTPA by CY30  
• Trades at ~13x FY28 EV/EBITDA

**HSBC on Adani Ports**

**Target Price: ₹1,950 (Raised)**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Strong growth visibility despite near-term caution  

• Q4 EBITDA grew ~20% YoY, beat estimates  
• FY27 guidance cautious amid Middle East disruptions  
• Targets ~18% EBITDA CAGR till FY31  
• ROCE expected around ~20% by FY31  
• ~$9 bn headroom available for M&A  
• TP raised on improved medium-term outlook

**Jefferies on Kotak Bank**

**Target Price: ₹450**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Healthy growth with improving credit trends  

• Q4 PAT ~₹40 bn, up ~13% YoY  
• Loan growth strong at ~16% YoY  
• NII grew ~8% YoY  
• CASA growth healthy at ~16%  
• Credit quality improving across segments  
• Earnings trimmed on lower NIM guidance

**Nomura on Kotak Bank**

**Target Price: ₹460**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Asset quality improvement driving earnings upgrades  

• Strong NIM performance in Q4  
• Asset quality improved sharply  
• FY27–28 EPS raised by ~2%  
• Lower credit cost and opex support growth  
• RoA/RoE seen at ~2%/12% by FY28  
• EPS CAGR estimated at ~18%

**JP Morgan on Kotak Bank**

**Target Price: ₹476**  
**Recommendation: Overweight**  

Strong core performance with steady margins  

• Q4 PAT ₹40.3 bn, up ~13% YoY  
• NII beat estimates; strong core performance  
• Opex lower and provisions declined sharply  
• Non-interest income remained muted YoY  
• NII grew ~8.1% YoY, among best peers  
• Core NIM stable; reported NIM up ~13 bps

**UBS on Kotak Bank**

**Target Price: ₹500**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Improving profitability with favourable risk-reward  

• PAT beat driven by higher PPOP  
• Lower credit cost supported earnings  
• Loan/deposit growth at 3.2%/5.5% QoQ  
• CASA ratio improved by ~200 bps QoQ  
• ECL transition impact below 2% net worth  
• Attractive valuation at ~1.5x FY27E P/BV

**Bernstein on Kotak Bank**

**Target Price: ₹500**  
**Recommendation: Market Perform**  

Strong operating performance but RoE still lags  

• Beat across most operating metrics in Q4  
• NIM expanded ~13 bps QoQ, peer-leading  
• Credit cost normalized to ~39 bps  
• Loan growth steady at ~16% YoY  
• Stable opex supported RoA above 2%  
• RoE at ~12.3%, below large peers

**DAM Capital on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹434**  
**Recommendation: Neutral**  

Growth moderate amid capex and tenancy concerns  

• Financials broadly in-line with estimates  
• Tower additions higher than estimates  
• Incremental tenancies relatively muted  
• Maintenance capex remains elevated  
• ARPT flat, key disappointment  
• Revenue/EBITDA/PAT CAGR at 8/6/6%  
• Valuation multiple cut to 7.0x

**Jefferies on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹370**  
**Recommendation: Underperform**  

Limited triggers with modest growth outlook  

• Q4 broadly in-line operationally  
• Dividend ₹14/share below expectations  
• EPS CAGR seen at ~3% FY26–29  
• Dividend yield below 4%  
• AGR clarity already priced in  
• Upcoming renewal risks remain concern

**HSBC on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹345**  
**Recommendation: Reduce**  

Weak FCF outlook and pricing pressure concerns  

• FCF and dividends below estimates  
• Capex increased ~28% YoY  
• Organic co-location additions declined YoY  
• Pricing power remains limited  
• Rentals likely to stay under pressure  
• Slower FCF growth key de-rating risk

**UBS on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹495**  
**Recommendation: Neutral**  

Mixed operational trends with capex concerns  

• Q4 results slightly soft vs expectations  
• Tower additions improved to ~4.9k  
• Tenancy additions strong at ~6.2k  
• Tenancy ratio flat at ~1.62x  
• Rentals declined QoQ across metrics  
• Capex increased to ₹23.3 bn  
• FY26 capex up ~28% YoY  
• Dividend ₹14/share below expectations

**Nomura on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹505 (Raised)**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Stable performance with catalysts ahead  

• Q4 stable; tenancy additions strong  
• Dividend reinstatement positive signal  
• Lower Vi AGR may aid fundraising  
• EBITDA dipped QoQ due to maintenance  
• Trades at ~6.3x FY28 EV/EBITDA  
• Vi debt raise progress key catalyst

**Citi on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹530**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Dividend return and tenancy growth key positives  

• Dividend resumed after four-year gap  
• ₹14/share equals ~100% FY26 FCF  
• Implies sustainable 4–5% yield outlook  
• Financial performance broadly in-line  
• Strong tenancy additions from Vodafone Idea  
• Higher contribution from Bharti Airtel  
• AGR clarity positive for business outlook

**CLSA on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹580**  
**Recommendation: Outperform (High Conviction)**  

Strong growth outlook with improving sector tailwinds  

• Q4 revenue ₹53 bn, in-line estimates  
• EBITDA up 6% YoY, flat QoQ  
• Record tenancy additions at ~6.2k  
• Added ~4.9k towers in Q4FY26  
• FY26 EBITDA grew ~11% YoY  
• AGR relief for Vodafone Idea supportive  
• Dividend reinstated at ₹14/share  
• Net cash position at ₹49 bn  
• Valuation attractive at ~6x EV/EBITDA

**UBS on Vodafone Idea**

**Target Price: ₹12.40**  
**Recommendation: Neutral**  

AGR relief supports valuation but risks remain  

• AGR dues revised to ₹640 bn  
• Payment schedule starts from FY32  
• Nominal payments in early years  
• Bulk repayment over FY36–41  
• Potential ~20% equity value upside  
• Funding and execution remain key monitor

**Citi on Vodafone Idea**

**Target Price: ₹14**  
**Recommendation: Buy (High Risk)**  

Regulatory clarity improves financial viability  

• AGR dues reassessed at ₹640 bn  
• ~20% lower vs earlier outstanding dues  
• No interest accrual improves liability economics  
• Effective AGR burden reduced on NPV basis  
• Majority dues payable over FY36–41  
• Regulatory uncertainty largely resolved  
• ₹250 bn debt raise now key trigger  
• ₹450 bn capex execution depends on funding

**CLSA on Vodafone Idea**

**Target Price: ₹11**  
**Recommendation: Outperform**  

AGR relief improves funding visibility  

• AGR dues cut to ₹640 bn from ₹877 bn  
• 10-year moratorium on AGR payments  
• Minimal payments from FY32–35  
• Larger instalments scheduled FY36–41  
• Spectrum debt remains high at ₹1,249 bn  
• Govt converted ₹370 bn debt to equity  
• Govt stake increased to ~49%  
• AGR clarity supports future fundraising plans

**Citi on JSPL**

**Target Price: ₹980 (Raised)**  
**Recommendation: Sell**  

Valuation rich despite improving operating metrics  

• Q4 EBITDA ~₹26.5 bn, up ~7% YoY  
• Volume growth strong at ~23% YoY  
• Realizations largely flat YoY  
• Costs increased ~3%, impacting margins  
• EBITDA/t at ~₹10,100 vs ₹11,650 YoY  
• Focus on utilization over value addition  
• Valuation at ~8.6x FY28 EV/EBITDA  
• Steel price correction poses downside risk

**Morgan Stanley on JSPL**

**Target Price: ₹1,250**  
**Recommendation: Overweight**  

Better realizations and volumes drive strong beat  

• EBITDA ₹29.4 bn, ~25% above estimates  
• Adjusted EBITDA ~13% above expectations  
• Volumes and realizations beat estimates  
• Opex/t lower than expected  
• Net debt increased to ₹160 bn  
• Net debt/EBITDA at ~1.66x  
• One-off impairment on Australia assets  
• FY27 outlook: higher realizations, rising coal costs

**CLSA on JSPL**

**Target Price: ₹1,420**  
**Recommendation: Outperform**  

Strong volume-led growth with robust outlook  

• Q4 EBITDA beat led by higher volumes  
• Volumes up 23% YoY in quarter  
• EBITDA/t improved to ₹10,103  
• Angul expansion to drive volume ramp-up  
• FY27 steel sales guided at 10.5–11 mt  
• Expect ~40% EBITDA CAGR FY26–28  
• Higher steel prices supporting near-term outlook  
• Capacity ramp-up execution remains key

**Jefferies on Avenue Supermarts**

**Target Price: ₹4,500 (Raised)**  
**Recommendation: Hold**  

Growth improving with expansion momentum  

• EBITDA grew ~25% YoY in Q4  
• LFL growth returned to double digits  
• Record store additions at 58 stores  
• Entered multiple new states in quarter  
• Temporary demand spike normalized by month-end  
• DMart Ready operations continue rationalisation

**Morgan Stanley on Avenue Supermarts**

**Target Price: ₹5,188**  
**Recommendation: Overweight**  

Strong LFL growth with steady margin improvement  

• Gross margin up 40 bps YoY  
• EBITDA margin improved 42 bps YoY  
• LFL growth at 10.8%, multi-quarter high  
• No supply chain disruptions observed  
• March demand spike normalized later

**CLSA on Avenue Supermarts**

**Target Price: ₹6,628**  
**Recommendation: Outperform**  

Strong operating leverage driving margin expansion  

• EBITDA grew 25% YoY in Q4  
• PAT growth stood at 17% YoY  
• Gross margins broadly in line estimates  
• EBITDA margin beat driven by cost leverage  
• Higher employee costs from store expansions  
• PAT lagged EBITDA due to lower other income

**Citi on Avenue Supermarts**

**Target Price: ₹3,650 (Raised)**  
**Recommendation: Sell**  

Improving demand but profitability and cash flow concerns  

• 4Q SSG improved to 10.8% QoQ  
• Revenue/PAT grew 19%/17% YoY  
• March demand spike normalized by month-end  
• Bill cuts per sq ft declined sequentially  
• Consolidated FCF negative at ₹9.6 bn  
• Net debt at ₹6.6 bn vs cash earlier  
• Profit lagging revenue in recent quarters  
• Competition and higher costs impacting margins
